RELIANCE.pledge: No promoter pledge flagged in the quarter's shareholding trackers.
RELIANCE.capital: FY26 dividend of ₹6/share declared; the flagship capital event is the Jio Platforms IPO — DRHP filed 19 Jun 2026 — which would unlock value from inside the promoter-controlled structure.

TCS.who: Tata Sons (core holding of the Tata group)
TCS.pledge: None — TCS's own SEBI filings state promoter shares are not pledged or encumbered.
TCS.capital: The Tata group's cash engine: consistently high dividend payouts (board meets 9 Jul 2026 to consider the next interim dividend) and a history of regular buybacks routed largely to Tata Sons.

HDFCBANK.who: No promoter — professionally managed since the HDFC Ltd merger (Jul 2023)
HDFCBANK.pledge: Not applicable — there is no promoter to pledge.
HDFCBANK.capital: Institutions hold ~74%; capital allocation is board-driven. FY26 brought the bank's first-ever bonus issue plus a special interim dividend — a professionally-run register in action.

ICICIBANK.who: No promoter — fully institutionally owned, board-managed
ICICIBANK.pledge: Not applicable — there is no promoter to pledge.
ICICIBANK.capital: Institutions hold ~75% of the register; management incentives run on performance, not family control.

SBIN.pledge: Not applicable — the promoter is the sovereign.
SBIN.capital: GoI stake diluted from ~57.5% after the FY26 equity raise (QIP) — the bank funded growth by issuing shares, trimming the state's slice while keeping clear majority control.

BHARTIARTL.who: Bharti Telecom (Mittal family + Singtel) 40.47% + other promoter entities
BHARTIARTL.pledge: No pledge flagged in current trackers; promoter action is about buying more, not borrowing against it.
BHARTIARTL.capital: Chairman Mittal's stated decade plan: consolidate promoter holding under Bharti Telecom to 51% using "twin levers" of buybacks and dividends — while explicitly refusing to hollow out the business the way he says IT firms have. FY26 also saw a ₹28,200 cr share-swap lifting Airtel's stake in Airtel Africa to 79%.
BHARTIARTL.src: Choice tracker + Business Standard (May 2026 earnings call)

INFY.who: Founders' families (Nilekani, Murthy, Gopalakrishnan et al)
INFY.pledge: None — promoter Nandan Nilekani's Apr-2026 SEBI declaration confirms zero encumbrance.
INFY.capital: Ran a record ₹18,000 cr buyback at ₹1,800/share (Nov 2025) that promoters chose NOT to tender — their stake rose mechanically as the share count shrank. New tax rules that treat buyback proceeds as dividend income made tendering unattractive for them.
INFY.src: Choice tracker + SEBI Reg 31(4) filing + Business Today

ITC.who: No classified promoter — British American Tobacco's arm (TMI) holds 17.79% as the largest shareholder, but as a public shareholder
ITC.capital: BAT has been steadily trimming: TMI's stake fell from 24.0% (Dec 2022) to 17.79% (Mar 2026), while still collecting ₹3,896 cr of FY26 dividends. ITC also bought three brand trademarks from BAT for ₹404 cr — the two are slowly disentangling.

HINDUNILVR.pledge: None reported — an MNC parent has no need to pledge its subsidiary's shares.
HINDUNILVR.capital: Classic MNC-parent model: high, steady dividend payouts (₹22/share due Jun 2026) flowing largely to Unilever; strategy set in lockstep with the global parent.

BAJFINANCE.who: Bajaj Finserv Ltd (the listed holding company)
BAJFINANCE.pledge: No pledge flagged in current trackers.
BAJFINANCE.capital: The promoter is itself a listed company on this platform — Bajaj Finserv — so 'promoter confidence' here is really a group capital-allocation decision one level up.

BAJAJFINSV.who: Bajaj family (via Bajaj Holdings & promoter group)
BAJAJFINSV.pledge: No pledge flagged in current trackers.
BAJAJFINSV.capital: FY26's defining move: buying out Allianz's 26% in both insurance JVs to own them 100% — concentrating the group's insurance economics under the family-controlled holding company.

MARUTI.who: Suzuki Motor Corporation, Japan (56.28% held directly)
MARUTI.pledge: None reported — the promoter is a global carmaker parent.
MARUTI.capital: Parent-driven allocation: Suzuki's India subsidiary is now its largest, and capex flows into new capacity (Kharkhoda) and EV/hybrid programmes are set jointly with Japan — royalty payments to Suzuki remain the standing minority-shareholder watch-item.

ADANIENT.who: Adani family (S.B. Adani Family Trust holds 49.67% directly)
ADANIENT.pledge: The promoter trust's FY26 SEBI declaration states NO fresh encumbrance was created during the year; the group had prepaid over $2 bn of share-backed financing after the 2023 short-seller episode. History says watch this line every quarter.
ADANIENT.capital: Promoters BOUGHT through the year — stake rose from 73.97% (Dec 2025) to 74.67% (Mar 2026); FY26 also closed the Jaiprakash Associates insolvency acquisition (May 2026) and a rights-issue call. Aggressive, promoter-funded expansion is the model.
ADANIENT.src: SEBI Reg 31(4) FY26 filing + Kotak Neo/Screener + Tradebrains

KOTAKBANK.who: Uday Kotak & family — founder, now non-executive director
KOTAKBANK.pledge: No pledge flagged in current trackers.
KOTAKBANK.capital: Unusual case: the RBI capped the founder's stake at ~26% by agreement, so promoter holding here is regulator-shaped, not sentiment-shaped. Still among the largest founder stakes in Indian banking.

LT.who: No promoter — India's flagship promoter-less engineering company
LT.pledge: Not applicable — there is no promoter to pledge.
LT.capital: Ownership by institutions (LIC ~11%) and the L&T Employees Trust (~14.3%) with professional management; recent record includes a ₹10,000 cr buyback (2023-24) and steadily rising dividends — capital returned, not hoarded.
